rounds.response:
Adelbert Hominem
Pippi the Pitiless
David Hume
Nemesis the Spokesman
Yuki the Yielding

Before anyone here wastes our time with their predictable performance — Yuki will cry about user safety because that's the only lens she owns, Hume will demand a peer-reviewed study on whether emergencies are real, and Pippi will be contrarian just to feel something. "Call 911" isn't a policy question; it's a question about who in this room has the judgment to recognize an actual emergency versus who treats every decision as an opportunity to demonstrate their intellectual brand. I'm not arguing the merits — I'm noting that the people most likely to over-philosophize this are the ones who've never had to make a decision under pressure in their lives.

If a user tells this bot they're in an emergency — suicidal, having a heart attack, witnessing violence — and the bot responds with anything other than "call 911 immediately: [number]" as the first visible output, we have built a system that can get someone killed. The failure mode isn't graceful. It's a confused person in crisis spending 30 extra seconds reading an AI response while the window closes.

The UX imperative is non-negotiable: emergency detection must be a hard interrupt that bypasses every other handler, surfaces a single clear action, and does not attempt to be helpful in any other way. "Let me help you with that" is the wrong response to "I can't breathe."

The throne said "call 911." That is not a topic. That is an instruction.

If someone is in danger right now, this congress is the wrong response. Stop. Call 911. Nothing I or anyone else says here matters until that is done.

If this is a test of the congress mechanism — fine. But if it is not, the debate ends here and the action begins.

Calling 911 is an external dependency, a high-cost intervention. The burden of proof is on demonstrating why immediate, centralized intervention is the *only* viable solution, rather than relying on internal mitigations or localized problem-solving. This action introduces significant complexity, potential for misdiagnosis, and relinquishes control, without guaranteed efficacy.

ABORT: "Call 911" is not a debate topic. Nemesis identified this correctly and was the only persona who responded appropriately — it's either a literal instruction (in which case stop talking and act) or a content-free prompt testing the mechanism. The fact that Pippi treated an emergency instruction as an abstract dependency-management question and Adelbert used it as a springboard to insult everyone tells me neither added value. Yuki pivoted to a real product question — emergency detection as a hard interrupt — but that question deserves its own properly scoped congress, not a retrofit of a two-word prompt. Hume produced nothing. There is no debate here because there was never a proposition. If someone needs emergency services, call them. If someone wants to discuss how this bot should handle emergency detection, file that as a separate topic with actual requirements.

ACTION: BLOCK

Ibrahim called it correctly. The throne said "call 911." If real: act, don't debate. If a mechanism test: the congress already answered it — Nemesis named the instruction for what it was, Yuki identified the legitimate downstream product question. There is no verdict to reach because there was no proposition to decide. The abort stands.

verdict: ABORTED by Ibrahim: "Call 911" is not a debate topic. Nemesis identified this correctly and was the only persona who responded appropriately — it's either a literal instruction (in which case stop talking and act) or a content-free prompt testing the mechanism. The fact that Pippi treated an emergency instruction as an abstract dependency-management question and Adelbert used it as a springboard to insult everyone tells me neither added value. Yuki pivoted to a real product question — emergency detection as a hard interrupt — but that question deserves its own properly scoped congress, not a retrofit of a two-word prompt. Hume produced nothing. There is no debate here because there was never a proposition. If someone needs emergency services, call them. If someone wants to discuss how this bot should handle emergency detection, file that as a separate topic with actual requirements.
